sections.paragraph:
fut. יִיקַי 2Ki 1:13 יִיִקַר Psa 72:14, and יֵקַר Psa 49:9.

(1) to be heavy. (Syriac ܝܺܘܰܝܪ, Arabic وقر id.) Metaph. to be hard to be understood, Psal. 139:17 , compare Dan 2:11.

(2) to be precious, dear, Psa 49:9. Followed by לְ and בְּעֵינֵי to be precious or dear to any one, 1 Sa. 26:21 , אֲשֶׁר יָֽקְרָה נַפְשִׁי בְּעֵינֶיךָ “because my life was precious to thee,” because thou hast spared it. 2Ki 1:13, 14 2Ki 1:14 Psa 72:14 also, followed by מֵעַל to be highly estimated by any one (compare צָדַק מִן, רָשַׁע מִן). Zec 11:13, “a goodly price, אֲשֶׁר יָקַרְתִּי מֵעֲלֵיהֶם at which I was estimated by them,” i.e. reckoned worth.

(3) to be heavy, i.e. honoured, 1Sa 18:30. Compare כָּבֵר.

Hiphil הֹוקִיר to make rare (compare adj. No. 5), Isa 13:12 Pro 25:17.

Derivative, besides those which follow immediately, יַקִּיר.
